model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With only 256 optimizer steps, cosine decay can reduce learning too aggressively; keeping a constant learning rate should improve progress within the fixed short horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, a larger batch processes more training examples and can improve validation loss without changing model or optimizer complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increasing batch size again should let the fixed 256-step run cover roughly a full epoch-plus of data, which can improve short-horizon fit on this setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After improving data coverage with larger batches, modestly increasing channel width should raise representational capacity and improve loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the larger batch and modestly wider network, a higher learning rate can improve optimization speed over the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous LR increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the stronger optimization setting before testing new ideas.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW can improve generalization versus Adam at the same learning rate and batch size by decoupling weight decay, which is promising for lowering validation loss.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW underperformed in the last run, so reverting to Adam restores the best-performing optimizer before trying other changes.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With short training and no augmentation/dropout, slightly reducing weight decay may improve fit and lower validation loss while keeping regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The reduced weight decay slightly worsened loss, so reverting to 1e-4 restores the stronger regularization setting.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider convolutional trunk may now be bottlenecked by the small MLP head; increasing hidden size is a low-risk capacity boost with limited extra compute.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous head-width increase gave a strong gain; pushing the classifier hidden size further is a simple continuation with low additional risk.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing the head to 128 regressed loss, so reverting to 64 restores the stronger prior configuration before further exploration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model/head capacity, a slightly lower learning rate may improve stability and final validation loss within the fixed update budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR reduction degraded validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the best-performing learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Test an intermediate batch size to balance sample coverage and optimization noise; 256 was strong, and 192 may yield a better tradeoff under 256 fixed steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The intermediate batch size regressed significantly, so reverting to 256 restores the strongest batch setting found so far.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A small additional width increase may further improve representation while staying close to the current strong configuration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the widened 14-channel model, a slight LR reduction may improve final-step convergence without deviating far from the current best setup.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}
